titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Garry Trudeau Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 9, 2019 at 04:03
Revised upon receipt of March 2018 acquisition.
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Garry Trudeau papers, spanning from 1967 to 2017, contain notebooks, research, correspondence, business papers, photographs, artwork, audiovisual materials, and writings. The papers also contain materials related to Garry Trudeau's work in theater and screen productions, several lines of merchandise, and various awards and recognitions.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 49, 61-62, 67 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 31, 38 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 58, 127 (record album storage): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 40-42, 36, 46, 63, 67 (videocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: October 2007 acquistion: gift of Garry Trudeau, 2007.
March 2018 acquisition: purchased from Doonesbury Ltd. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: October 2007 acquisition arranged in chronological order.

March 2018 acquisition organized into eight series: I. Notebooks, 1982-2015. II. Correspondence, 1970-2008. III. Business Papers, 1970-2010. IV. Personal Papers, 1967-2013. V. Artwork and Writings, 1971-2006. VI. Theater and Screen Productions, 1977-2015. VII. Merchandise, 1976-2010. VIII. Awards and Recognition, 1975-2017.

bioghist.head: Garry Trudeau
bioghist.p: Garry Trudeau was born in New York City in 1948, and was raised in Saranac Lake, New York. He received a B.A. and M.F.A. in graphic design from Yale University. In 1970, he launched the comic strip Doonesbury , which (as of 2007) appears in over one thousand newspapers worldwide. His work has also appeared in the form of books, films, and theater musicals.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Garry Trudeau Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Garry Trudeau papers, spanning from 1967 to 2017, contain notebooks, research, correspondence, business papers, and artwork related to Doonesbury. The papers also contain materials related to Garry Trudeau's work in theater and screen productions, journalism, fan letters, merchandise, and awards.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Garry Trudeau Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The October 2007 acquisition consists of forty-two notebooks, dating from 1969 to 2007, which contain notes and sketches documenting the creation of Doonesbury , other cartoons, and related work.
Former call number: Uncat MSS 986.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The March 2018 acquisition contains notebooks, research, correspondence, business papers, photographs, artwork, audiovisual materials, and writings. Also included are materials related to Garry Trudeau's work in theater and screen productions, several lines of merchandise, and various awards and recognitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Garry Trudeau's notebooks with notes and sketches with ideas for Doonesbury strips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two journals dated as follows: 1 February 1982; Febraury 1985.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five notebooks dated as follows: 1 June 1986; mid-January 1987 - 22 September 1993; August 1989; April 1990; early March 1991.
Notebook dated mid-January 1987 - 22 September 1993 was used by Trudeau when he was between other notebooks, hence the wider date range.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four notebooks dated as follows: 18 October 1993; 25 May 1995 ; early April 1996; December 1998.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five notebooks dated as follows: 13 October 2003 - 15 May 2004; December 2004; July 2005; September 2006; June 3, 2007.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five notebooks dated as follows: 20 January 2008; 21 October 2008; 14 May 2009; 10 October [2010]; June 2011.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four notebooks dated as follows:17 January 2012; 12 September 2012; August 2013; 5 January 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains both written correspondence and printed emails. Also includes printed comment sections from multiple websites, photographs, and related materials.

did.unittitle: Correspondence related to the New York Times
Correspondence related to research about U.S. veteran experience

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence related to research of U.S. veteran experience Trudeau conducted to inform his portrayal of Doonesbury character B.D.'s amputation and subsequent PTSD.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers related to collaborative projects with various organizations, primarily involving Doonesbury. Also contains research for articles, materials related to publicity, and press passes from both Garry Trudeau and Jane Pauley.

did.unittitle: Starbucks 2000: The concept for the new millenium

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in seven of the eight folders were originally in three binders labelled with different dates: 1991, 1992, and 1993-1995. Folders are organized chronologically and by the original order within these binders (for example, "1992, folder 1 of 2").

did.unittitle: Andy and AIDS: The strips and responses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials produced for Yale University and Trinity College.

did.unittitle: Papers related to Calligraph printing firm
Materials relating to American Samoa piece
Materials relating to Loch Ness monster search
Materials related to the Great Doonesbury Sellout
Press passes for China Today, Jane Pauley
Press passes, Garry Trudeau and Jane Pauley
Photographs from professional events and meetings
Materials related to Quality Paperback Club
Materials related to Doonesbury's Joanie applying to law school

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

DVD of Charlie Rose with Garry Trudeau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies of the same DVD.

DVD of Up Close Garry Trudeau interview, off air copy
DVD-R of CBS segment with Garry Trudeau

CD of Doonesburyland, BBC Radio 4 segment

CD of Phill Jupitus' Comic Love, BBC Radio 4 segment
CDs from Doonesbury 25th anniversary gift set

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to Garry Trudeau's travels around the world, including a trip to the Middle East during Operation Desert Storm, as well as his attendance at various political conferences. Also contains essays and other assignments from Garry Trudeau's undergraduate career at Yale.

did.unittitle: Papers related to Jimmy Thudpucker record
Papers related to Desert Storm visit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder is titled "Desert Storm (2)," but no "Desert Storm (1)" was present.
This folder was originally adjacent to the "Desert Shield" materials and may be related.

did.unittitle: Materials relating to National Soap Box Derby
Slides relating to National Soap Box Derby
Metal plate from T55 tank, Desert Storm visit
Embroidered patch from Desert Storm visit
Notes and papers related to Desert Storm visit
Photograph albums from Desert Storm visit
Handwritten notebook in Arabic from Desert Storm visit
Materials related to White House visit
Clipping of "I Remember the Sixties" by R. Crumb
West Wing script signed by Martin Sheen
Pieces of mail and blank postcard
Materials related to Millicent H. Fenwick's death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a printout of a eulogy for Millicent H. Fenwick dated September 19, 1992, along with a used pipe and book of matches bearing Millicent's name.

did.unittitle: Military rations (MRE) and desert camouflage hot sauce holster
Personal papers electronic and analog media

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings and copies of Garry Trudeau's written pieces for multiple publications. Also contains pieces of artwork from Garry Trudeau for professional and personal projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers, recordings, photographs, and notes related to Garry Trudeau's theatrical productions and television productions.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains merchandise of Doonesbury, including materials from a collaborative project with Starbucks, computer games, records, clothing, and figurines. Also includes t-shirts with art from Garry Trudeau for various events and organizations, many local to the New England area.

did.unittitle: Photograph related to Jimmy Thudpucker record
T-shirt for American Lung Association Ride for Research
T-shirt for New Haven Road Race
T-shirt for World Hunger Year 25th anniversary
T-shirt for Doonesbury Musical Out of Town, gray
T-shirt for Doonesbury Musical Out of Town, white
Mug for New Haven Road Race
Ice cream container for Ben & Jerry's Doonesberry flavor
Doonesbury Uncle Duke action figure and book
Necktie and greeting card for Starbucks
Products for the Friends of Museum Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four t-shirts, a sweatshirt, buttons and magnets, a tie, and a large tote bag.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 15 t-shirts

did.unittitle: Products for the Great Doonesbury Sellout

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three handkerchiefs, seven rubber stamps, one inkpad, five baseball caps, a case of pins, a case of earrings, a case of buttons, a Duke pin, a leather wallet, and a leather CD case.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains awards and printed recognition presented to Garry Trudeau, including honorary degrees from over 30 different institutions. Also includes papers related to commencement ceremonies where Garry Trudeau was a guest and speaker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from the editors of 29 newspapers congratulating Trudeau on Doonesbury's 20th anniversary. These 29 newspapers were Doonesbury's initial clients at its launch in 1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, printed drawings, and correspondence compiled into a scrapbook for Doonesbury's 35th anniversary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two plaques and three trophies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four plaques and one medal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Immigration Lawyers award is in three glass pieces: base, plaque, and decorative blue globe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Award is glass, very heavy with sharp edges.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four plaques. One plaque has a case, and one plaque has a separate sculpted component wrapped with it.

did.unittitle: Materials related to awards and certificates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four plaques.

did.unittitle: Papers related to Academy Award nomination
Papers related to Western Maryland College Commencements
Recognition from the state of Wisconsin
Recognition and stamps from the United Nation Postal Administration
Materials related to Doonesbury 35th anniversary party
Recognition from the crew of the U.S. Space Shuttle Discovery
